title: Who Is the Dear Friend of Muslims?
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source confirms that the OCR's extra opening line is spurious and that `Ganga` replaces `தேர்தல்` (election). The duplicated junk is omitted from the derived Tamil reading copy, and source-confirmed or unambiguous page-break and character errors are repaired there. All policy dates, percentages and beneficiary figures are retained. The canonical Tamil chapter remains unchanged.
paragraphs: Every newspaper has reported Chief Minister Jayalalithaa's speech at an election campaign meeting in Nagercoil on 9 March 2014. Her party's official newspaper carried it under the headline: "Deceiving, defrauding and betraying people over reservation is an art Karunanidhi has mastered; it was the government under my leadership that granted reservation to Muslims."

Chief Minister Jayalalithaa claims that it was the AIADMK government that granted reservation to Muslims. We say it was granted under the DMK government. Which is true? If it was granted under AIADMK rule, was legislation enacted? On what date was the order issued? In which newspaper was the news published? Is Jayalalithaa prepared to answer precisely and specifically?

When the DMK won and formed the government in 2006, did not the Governor's Address read on 24 May 2006 itself state: "The necessary legislative amendment will be introduced to provide reservation in education and employment to Muslims and Christians belonging to minority communities"? Does that statement remain in the Address even today, or does it not?

Jayalalithaa says the AIADMK government reconstituted the State Backward Classes Commission in 2006 and added the requirement that reservation be granted only after obtaining its recommendation, and therefore it was her government that granted reservation to Muslims. I ask the distinguished members of the Muslim community. I ask the comrades of the various movements who still say they support Jayalalithaa's alliance. Do you accept that what Jayalalithaa did amounted to granting reservation?

Jayalalithaa was Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu from 2001 to 2006. If she genuinely believed Muslims should receive reservation, could she not have obtained the Backward Classes Commission's recommendation during those five years and granted it? Why did she not do so?

It was in my announcement under the DMK government on 13 September 2007 that I said: "The 2006–07 Budget announced that, while urging the Union Government to provide separate reservation in educational institutions and employment for Muslims and Christians belonging to minority communities, this government would take firm measures needed to make such reservation possible in Tamil Nadu. As the first step towards implementing that announcement, the government asked the Tamil Nadu Backward Classes Commission, headed by Justice Janarthanam, to conduct a detailed inquiry and study and submit its recommendation. Accepting that recommendation, and as a gift on Perarignar Anna's ninety-ninth birthday, an ordinance is being issued granting, from 15 July 2007, separate reservation in Tamil Nadu's educational institutions and employment: 3.5 per cent for Muslims included in the State's Backward Classes list and 3.5 per cent for Christians included in that list, carved out of the thirty per cent reservation for Backward Classes." Every newspaper reported this at the time.

Yet at Nagercoil, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa claimed that the AIADMK granted reservation to Muslims. She went further and said that deception, fraud and betrayal were arts I had mastered. Anyone else might speak of betrayal, fraud and deception, poor souls, but can Jayalalithaa? Who deceived, defrauded and betrayed the Left parties that trusted her until the day before yesterday?
Jayalalithaa also said at Nagercoil that only the AIADMK government had benefited minorities. Here is the list of benefits the DMK government provided Muslims:
The Directorate of Minorities Welfare was created under the DMK government and has functioned since 1 August 2007.
Accepting a longstanding demand of the Muslim community, the DMK government established the Urdu Academy in Tamil Nadu on 21 July 2000.
From 2007–08, scholarships supported by Union Government funding were given to minority students pursuing vocational and technical education. Under this scheme, 7,090 students received ₹18.53 crore.
From 2007–08, the DMK government also provided Union-supported scholarships to minority students from Class XI through research studies. Under this scheme, 67,683 students received ₹22.22 crore.

In 2008–09, the DMK government introduced scholarships, funded jointly by the Union and State governments in a 75:25 ratio, for minority students from Classes I to X. In the first phase, scholarships were secured and distributed to 15,340 students in Tamil Nadu. Because the State government created extensive awareness among minorities, ₹18.90 crore was given to 1,54,264 students in 2009–10 and ₹28.62 crore to 2,42,301 students in 2010–11.

To help unemployed minority youth obtain private-sector jobs and improve their skills, the DMK government introduced a specialised technical-training programme from 2007–08 in information technology, garment design and production, footwear design and related fields. Supported by Tamil Nadu government funds, it began at a cost of ₹2.50 crore for women and young people. Over four and a half years, 41,316 people benefited at a cost of ₹10 crore.

Speaking at Nagercoil, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa proudly mentioned raising the monthly pension for ulema to ₹1,000. But the Ulema and Other Personnel Welfare Board was established under the DMK government on 24 August 2009 with an allocation of ₹1 crore. Identity cards were issued to 11,274 members, and welfare assistance costing ₹12.93 lakh was provided to 704 beneficiaries.

Under the ulema pension scheme, the pension of ₹200 was raised by the DMK government to ₹400 in 2006 and to ₹500 a month on 1 December 2010. The AIADMK government later raised that amount further. Under the DMK, the number of pension recipients rose from 2,000 in 1998–99 to 2,200, and then to 2,400 in 2007–08. The DMK government also extended the ulema pension scheme to mujavars serving in dargahs.

To safeguard the welfare and rights of religious and linguistic minorities, the first State Minorities Commission was established under the DMK government on 13 December 1989 and began functioning on 14 March 1990.

With the worthy purpose of protecting minority rights, the DMK government enacted the Tamil Nadu State Minorities Commission Act, 2010, granting statutory recognition to the Commission. A government order authorised it to exercise statutory powers from 1 August 2010, and the Commission began functioning under Reverend Vincent Chinnadurai with five members.

Immediately after assuming office in 2006, the DMK government repealed the anti-conversion law introduced by the previous AIADMK government. It also returned the one day's salary that the previous government had withheld from every teacher who joined the protest against that law.

The Tamil Nadu Minorities Economic Development Corporation was established under the DMK government on 27 December 1999 to advance the economic welfare of minorities and continues to function. During the previous DMK government, it provided individual loans totalling ₹29.68 crore to 7,208 members of minority communities.

Over five years, automobile loans totalling ₹10,67,85,000 were given to 966 people. Loans of ₹27.94 lakh were given to 146 people to purchase milch cows. Small-business loans totalling ₹46,46,90,000 were given to 34,589 people.

The Saudi government determines each country's Hajj quota according to its Muslim population. From that allocation, States receive quotas proportionate to their Muslim populations under the 2001 Census. Because applications exceeded Tamil Nadu's quota during DMK rule, the government requested an additional allocation from the Prime Minister. In response, the Union Government enabled 5,611 Muslims from Tamil Nadu to undertake the Hajj over five years in addition to the prescribed quota of 13,626. It was the DMK government that stood beside them.

M. S. A. Siddique, chairman of the Union Government's National Commission for Minority Educational Institutions, and the delegation he led reviewed Tamil Nadu's minority-welfare schemes. Fully satisfied with the DMK government's programmes, they praised it as a "government that works for minorities," a pro-minority government.

Is that all the DMK government did for Muslims? In 1969, it declared Milad-un-Nabi a government holiday for the first time. After the previous AIADMK government cancelled it in 2001, an order dated 15 November 2006 restored the holiday.

It was under DMK rule in 1973 that Urdu-speaking Muslims were included in the Backward Classes list. In 1974, the Government Women's College on Anna Salai in Chennai was named Quaid-e-Millath Government College for Women. In 2001, the DMK government laid the foundation stone on 25 February for the Quaid-e-Millath Memorial in Chennai, costing ₹58.69 lakh, and made arrangements for its completion and opening.

Jayalalithaa now speaks in support of Muslims at election time. What did she say about them in the past? Asked whether she supported building a Ram temple at Ayodhya, she replied: "Yes, I support it. If a Ram temple cannot be built in India itself, where else can it be built?"

In this Lok Sabha election, the Indian Union Muslim League and the Manithaneya Makkal Katchi are allied with the DMK and contest one seat each. Has the AIADMK given even one seat to any Muslim movement? The DMK has fielded two Muslim brothers, and allied parties another two, making four candidates, or ten per cent. The AIADMK has given an opportunity to only one. Looking at this, who deceives, defrauds and betrays Muslims over reservation: Karunanidhi or Jayalalithaa?

The uninterrupted association and relationship between the Muslim community, our Dravidian movement and me did not arise yesterday or today. Seventy-five years ago, while still a schoolboy, I participated in an Indian Union Muslim League conference in Tiruvarur as a boy carrying its green crescent flag. I remember it with pride.

Khan Bahadur Kalifulla Sahib, then Diwan of Pudukkottai, presided over that conference. In one hand I carried Kudi Arasu; in the other, Darul Islam, a journal campaigning for the Muslim League. Those were the adornments of my hands in those days.

The relationship and feeling that budded within me as a student have grown lush and green today. From my youth, I participated in countless Muslim functions, meetings and conferences. Whenever I assumed responsibility for government, I unfailingly discharged my duties to that community.

One incident feels as though it happened yesterday. It moved my heart and has remained forever in my memory. Quaid-e-Millath Ismail Sahib, leader of the Indian Union Muslim League, recipient of Periyar's affection, Perarignar Anna's love and my boundless respect, was admitted to Stanley Hospital in Chennai for treatment in April 1972. As soon as I learned that his condition was critical, I cancelled my tour of Coimbatore district and immediately returned to Chennai.

When I saw his condition at Stanley Hospital, I lacked the strength to bear the grief. He lay unconscious with his eyes closed. I bent near him and said softly, "Ayya, I am Karunanidhi. I have come." Hearing my voice, Quaid-e-Millath opened his eyes.

Tenkasi N. K. Rifayi recorded what followed in his book Guardian of the Community: "He opened his eyes. He understood who had come. On seeing Kalaignar Karunanidhi, he extended his hands, and Kalaignar held them. In a very weak voice, Quaid-e-Millath said, 'My thanks for all the assistance you have rendered to the Muslim community.' On hearing this, Kalaignar was overwhelmed. His eyes filled with tears. He struggled, not knowing what to say. In the meantime, Quaid-e-Millath's eyes closed and he again lost consciousness. He never regained consciousness after that."

As a continuation of the respect I had for Muslims from my youth, and as a sign of my love and affection for Quaid-e-Millath, I have provided many benefits to the Muslim community whenever the opportunity arose. Yet Jayalalithaa says that I deceive and defraud them.

"Islam is a way of life," Anna said. The Prophet taught principles of life applicable not only to Muslims but to all humanity. He said everyone should treat others with affection and build relationships. Teaching people to abandon the habit of finding fault with others, he said: "Do not roam in search of others' faults. Do not lower others merely to raise yourself. Do not envy others. Do not speak ill of others behind their backs. Do not turn your face away from them."

I hope that Jayalalithaa, who has spoken about Muslims, will at least keep these teachings of the Prophet in mind and conduct her campaign more graciously hereafter. We must remind Muslims of every benefit the DMK government provided them. We must explain that Jayalalithaa once gave an interview saying, "Muslims alone are not minorities; there are other communities too," and that it was the DMK government that granted reservation to Muslims.
With affection,
M. K.
15 March 2014
